The Real Problem

Your AC Is Not The Problem. The Way Most Homeowners Think About It Is.

Texas heat does not care and it gives no warning. When your AC fails at 11pm on a Saturday night in August with the kids at home, you are not thinking about tune-ups or maintenance plans — you are panicking. The homeowners who never have that moment are not lucky but smart. They just called BlueSky before summer started.

🌡️ Fear of Breakdown In Peak Heat

Garland summers average 30+ days above 100°F. An AC failure is not an inconvenience — it is a health emergency for elderly and young children. The average emergency repair call in July takes 48-72 hours just to schedule.

⚡ Electricity Bills That Keep Climbing

A dirty or under-maintained AC works harder to cool the same space — driving your electricity bill up 20-30% without your knowing why. One annual tune-up typically saves more than it costs in reduced energy use alone.

Being Oversold By The Big Companies

Large HVAC companies make large chunks of their money from replacements — not repairs. Many Garland homeowners have been told they need a full system replacement when a $200 repair would have fixed the problem. Once trust is gone, it does not come back.

💸 High Repair Costs No One Saw Coming

The difference between a $99 tune-up and a $6,000 emergency system replacement is usually six months of ignored maintenance. Small problems become expensive ones when they are caught late — or not at all.

⏳ Not Knowing When To Repair Vs. Replace

Is your 11-year-old system worth repairing? Or should you invest in a new high-efficiency unit? Without honest advice from someone who is not trying to sell you something, these decisions are stressful and expensive to get wrong.

🌫️ Indoor Air Quality Nobody Talks About

The air inside your home can be 2 to 5 times more polluted than outside — especially in Texas where homes stay sealed against heat for months. Dust buildup in duct systems, mold, and poor filtration affects your family's health quietly and consistently.

The BlueSky Difference

Honest Service From A Family That Lives In The Same Heat You Do.

Mike Torres started BlueSky Air Comfort in 2008 with one van and a simple promise: tell homeowners the truth, charge them fairly, be reliable, and show up when they call. Seventeen years later, that is still the only way we know how to work.

🚨

Same Day Emergency Service

When your AC dies, every hour matters. We prioritise emergency calls and work evenings and weekends because Texas heat does not check the calendar.

🏆

SEER2-Standard High Efficiency Equipment

Carrier and Lennox authorised dealer. We install and service only top-quality systems that meet or exceed current federal efficiency standards — keeping your electricity bills lower long term.

📋

NATE-Certified & TACL Licensed

Every technician on our team is experienced and holds current NATE certification, Texas state TACL licensing, and EPA 608 certification. Not every HVAC company in Garland can say that.

🏠

Locally Owned & Operated

Mike's kids go to school here. Sandra grew up in Garland. This is not a franchise from out of state — we are neighbours who know and care about what happens in our neighbourhood.

🚫

Zero Overpricing. Ever.

Before we touch anything, we give you the exact cost in writing. We never charge beyond the agreed price — not by a dollar. What we quote is what you pay.

📅

17 Years In Garland

We have serviced homes through Texas winters, record summer heat waves, and post-hail rush seasons. Experience is not something we claim — it is something our customers vouch for.

How It Works

Four Steps From Your First Call To A Home That Stays Cool All Summer.

1

You Call or Book Online — We Pick Up

Sandra answers every call personally. No call centres, no hold music, no automated menus. You tell us what you need and we schedule you at a time that actually works for your day — including evenings and weekends.

2

Mike or Dave Arrives On Time — In Uniform, With ID

Our technicians arrive in marked BlueSky vans, on time, with full identification. We do a thorough diagnostic inspection before we recommend anything — because we need to understand your system before we give you any advice.

3

We Tell You Exactly What We Find — And What It Will Cost

No surprise charges. No work done without your approval. We show you what we find, explain in plain language clearly what it means, and give you an exact quote before we touch anything. You decide — no pressure from us.

4

The Job Is Done Right — And We Follow Up

We clean up completely before we leave. Sandra calls you the next day to make sure everything is working as it should. If anything is not right, we come back — no argument, no visiting charges.

What You Actually Get

Stop Hoping Your AC Survives Another Texas Summer. Make Sure It Does.

Smart Garland homeowners do not wait for problems to find them. They call BlueSky in March — before the heat arrives, before the emergency slots fill up, before the panic kicks in. Here is what that decision gives you:

✓

A Cool Home On The Hottest Days

A properly maintained system runs at full efficiency even during a 107°F Garland afternoon — because it has been checked, cleaned, and serviced before that day arrived.

✓

Lower Electricity Bills Every Month

A clean, tuned system uses 15-25% less electricity than a neglected one doing the same job. The tune-up pays for itself — usually within the first two months of summer.

✓

Priority Emergency Response

Maintenance plan members go to the front of the line. During peak season when most companies are booked 3 days out, your call gets answered the same day. Loyalty is appreciated and rewarded.

✓

Cleaner Indoor Air Quality

We inspect and clean your filtration system, check your duct system for buildup and mold, and advise you on the right filter type for your home and your family's health needs.

✓

Honest Repair vs. Replace Advice

When your system is aging, we give you the real numbers — repair cost versus replacement cost versus expected remaining lifespan — and let you decide. No pressure. No hidden agenda.

✓

One Company That Knows Your System

When you are a BlueSky customer, we maintain your system's history, your equipment age, your previous repairs. Every visit builds on the last one — no starting from scratch every time.

About BlueSky

Built In Garland. Run By A Family That Lives Here Too.

I started BlueSky in 2008 with a used service van, a TACL license, and eight years of experience working under the best HVAC tech I have ever known. No investors. No franchise. Just a maxed-out credit card and a belief that Garland homeowners deserved better than what the big companies were promising and actually giving them.

My wife Sandra runs our office and answers every call herself — because nobody should be on hold when their AC is out in July. My technicians Dave and Ricky know your equipment like it is their own. We are not a big company. We do not want to be one. What we want is to be the only company you ever call, trust completely, and never have a reason to leave.

If your unit has two good years left, I will tell you that. If a $150 repair will do what a salesman is trying to charge you $4,000 for, you will hear it from me first. That is the only way I know how to run a business.

🛡️

Our Guarantee Is Simple — And It Is Real.

Every BlueSky service comes with a 90-day workmanship guarantee. If any repair or maintenance work we perform fails within 90 days due to our workmanship, we come back and fix it at no charge. No argument. No service call fee. No paperwork. Additionally, all equipment we install carries the full manufacturer's warranty — Carrier and Lennox both offer industry-leading parts coverage on all installed equipment. If something is not right, you call us. That is it.

Common Questions

Honest Answers To What Most Garland Homeowners Ask.

Q1. How do I know if I need a repair or a full replacement?
The honest answer depends on three things — your system's age, the cost of the repair, and your current energy bills. As a general rule, if the repair costs more than 50% of a new system and your unit is over 12 years old, replacement makes more financial sense. We will give you both numbers clearly, show you the math, and let you decide. We have no incentive to steer you either way — our business runs on referrals, not commission.
Q2. Do you charge a service call fee just to come out?
Yes, we charge a diagnostic fee of $99 for all service calls. This covers the cost of our technician's time to properly diagnose your system. If you proceed with the repair, that $99 is credited toward your total invoice. Maintenance plan members receive waived diagnostic fees on covered visits.
Q3. How quickly can you get to my home in an emergency?
For maintenance plan members, same-day emergency service is our commitment during business hours and next-morning priority after hours. For non-plan customers during peak summer season, typical response is 24-48 hours. Call us directly and tell us your situation — we will do what we can.
Q4. What brands of equipment do you install and service?
We are authorised Carrier and Lennox dealers, and we service all major brands including Trane, Daikin, Goodman, Rheem, and American Standard. Our preference is Carrier and Lennox because we know those systems inside and out and the manufacturer support in our area is excellent — but we will always give you an honest comparison if you are choosing between brands.
Q5. Is my equipment under warranty and what does it cover?
Most new Carrier and Lennox systems come with a 10-year parts warranty when registered properly after installation — something we handle for every customer we install for. Labor warranties vary. Our 90-day workmanship guarantee covers our labor on everything we touch. If you are unsure about your existing warranty status, call Sandra and she can help you look it up at no charge.
Q6. Can I trust a small company for a large system replacement?
That is a fair question and one we respect you for asking. BlueSky has completed hundreds of full system replacements in Garland and surrounding areas over 17 years. We carry full liability insurance, all required state licensing, and we pull permits for every installation where required by code. The advantage of a small company is accountability — when Mike installs your system, Mike is the one who answers when you call. You will never be transferred to a call centre.
Do Not Wait Until July

Spring Maintenance Slots Are Filling Up. Once They Are Gone, They Are Gone.

Every year, the same thing happens. Homeowners wait until June to call us about a tune-up — and by then, our spring slots are already full and the summer emergency queue has started. The homeowners who call in March and April get the dates they want, the peace of mind they need, and the lower pre-season rates that are not available once demand spikes.
